title: ContextScore
description: The computed context-readiness score for a single domain. Produced by `prevail score <domain>`. The headline `score` is the 0-100 roll-up; `breakdown` explains it across six fixed dimensions; `missing` lists actionable gaps.

detail.description: Short human explanation of why this dimension scored as it did.

ScoreBreakdown.description: The six fixed scoring dimensions. The set and order are FROZEN — never add, remove, or rename a dimension without a schema-version bump.

coverage.description: How many of the expected domain files exist (state.md, decisions.md, open-loops.md, MEMORY.md, manifest.json, ...).

density.description: How much real content the knowledge files hold vs. empty scaffolding.

freshness.description: How recently the domain's writable knowledge was updated.

structure.description: How well the markdown is structured (headings, Open Items section, parseable shape).

activity.description: Recent chat/log/journal activity volume in this domain.

config_completeness.description: How fully manifest.json identity/config/goals/routing are filled in.

domain.description: Domain name (directory name under the vault root).

score.description: Headline 0-100 context-readiness score, a weighted roll-up of the six breakdown dimensions.

missing.description: Actionable gaps. Empty array when the domain is fully formed.

freshness_secs.description: Seconds since the domain's most-recently-modified writable knowledge file was touched.

assessment.description: Optional LLM-written narrative assessment. null when the score is purely heuristic (no --audit was run).

audit_source.description: Identifier of the engine that produced the LLM `assessment` (e.g. 'claude:opus-4-8'). null when no audit ran.

computed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the heuristic score was computed.

audited_at.description: Epoch milliseconds when the LLM audit ran, or null if never audited.
